How can community engagement contribute to the long-term fight against extremism in society? Title: Interview with Security Expert Dr. Imran Rahman‚Äç on ‚Å¢Recent Success Against Fitnah Al-Khawarij
Interviewer (Time.news Editor): Good morning, everyone! Today, we have a very special guest with us, Dr. Imran Rahman, a distinguished expert in‚Äã security studies‚Å§ and‚Äå counter-terrorism. ‚ÄçWe‚Äôll be discussing the recent operation against the extremist group Fitnah ‚ÄãAl-Khawarij, which has drawn attention from top officials including Prime Minister‚Äå Shahbaz Sharif, President Asif Ali Zardari,‚Äå and Federal Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi. Dr.‚Å§ Imran ‚Å§Rahman: Good morning! It‚Äôs a pleasure ‚Äçto be ‚Å§here.
Editor: Let‚Äôs dive right in. Recently, the security forces have been commended for their successful operation against Fitnah Al-Khawarij. What can you tell us about this group and ‚Äçthe nature of the threat it poses?
Dr. Rahman: Fitnah Al-Khawarij is a takfiri extremist ‚Äågroup that has gained notoriety for its radical ideology‚Äç and violent actions. A successful operation instills confidence in the public and‚Å¢ enhances trust in the security apparatus. Dr. ‚Å£Rahman: Absolutely. For long-term solutions, we must focus on education, job creation, and community ‚Äçresilience programs.‚Å£ Engaging youth and offering them ‚Äåalternatives to extremist ideologies ‚Äåis vital. ‚Å§Additionally, promoting interfaith dialog and ensuring that marginalized groups feel included in the nation-building‚Å¢ process can significantly reduce fanaticism.

Dr. Rahman: The ‚Äãmessage‚Å§ should‚Äã be‚Å£ one of hope and unity. We ‚Å£need to emphasize that while the fight against extremism is ongoing, it is ‚Å£a‚Äç collective effort. The public‚Äôs ‚Å¢role in reporting suspicious activities, supporting ‚Å§peace‚Äç initiatives, and ‚Äãparticipating in community building cannot be overstated.
